Traffic Light Controller
  • top
  • traffic_light
  • traffic_light — Entity
  • View page source

traffic_light — Entity

Source file: src/traffic_light.vhd.

ENTITY traffic_light IS

Traffic light controller.

Implements a standard UK traffic light sequencer with four states: RED, RED_AMBER, GREEN, and AMBER. The FSM advances on each rising clock edge when timer_exp is asserted.

State sequence: RED -> RED_AMBER -> GREEN -> AMBER -> RED.

Generics

Generic

Type

Default

Description
Ports

Port

Type

Mode

Description

clk

std_logic

in

System clock, rising-edge triggered.

rst

std_logic

in

Synchronous active-high reset. Returns FSM to RED state.

timer_exp

std_logic

in

Phase timer expiry pulse. Must be high for exactly one cycle.

red_out

std_logic

out

Red lamp drive. Asserted in RED and RED_AMBER states.

amber_out

std_logic

out

Amber lamp drive. Asserted in RED_AMBER and AMBER states.

green_out

std_logic

out

Green lamp drive. Asserted in GREEN state only.

Annotated Source

src/traffic_light.vhd
  1library ieee;
  2use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
  3use ieee.numeric_std.all;
  4
  5-- Traffic light controller.
  6--
  7-- Implements a standard UK traffic light sequencer with four states:
  8-- RED, RED_AMBER, GREEN, and AMBER. The FSM advances on each rising
  9-- clock edge when timer_exp is asserted.
 10--
 11-- State sequence: RED -> RED_AMBER -> GREEN -> AMBER -> RED.
 12entity traffic_light is
 13    port (
 14        -- System clock, rising-edge triggered.
 15        clk       : in  std_logic;
 16        -- Synchronous active-high reset. Returns FSM to RED state.
 17        rst       : in  std_logic;
 18        -- Phase timer expiry pulse. Must be high for exactly one cycle.
 19        timer_exp : in  std_logic;
 20        -- Red lamp drive. Asserted in RED and RED_AMBER states.
 21        red_out   : out std_logic;
 22        -- Amber lamp drive. Asserted in RED_AMBER and AMBER states.
 23        amber_out : out std_logic;
 24        -- Green lamp drive. Asserted in GREEN state only.
 25        green_out : out std_logic
 26    );
 27end entity traffic_light;
 28
 29-- RTL implementation using a 3-process Moore FSM style.
 30architecture rtl of traffic_light is
 31
 32    -- FSM state type encoding all four traffic light phases.
 33    type t_state is (
 34        -- Red light only. Vehicles must remain stopped.
 35        RED,
 36        -- Red and amber simultaneously. Vehicles prepare to move.
 37        RED_AMBER,
 38        -- Green light. Vehicles may proceed.
 39        GREEN,
 40        -- Amber light only. Vehicles prepare to stop.
 41        AMBER
 42    );
 43
 44    -- Current state register, initialised to RED at power-on.
 45    signal state      : t_state := RED;
 46    -- Next state combinational signal, feeds state register.
 47    signal next_state : t_state;
 48
 49begin
 50
 51    -- p_state_reg: Clocked state register.
 52    --
 53    -- Captures next_state on each rising edge of clk.
 54    -- When rst is asserted the state returns to RED synchronously,
 55    -- regardless of timer_exp or next_state.
 56    --
 57    -- .. wavedrom::
 58    --
 59    --    { "signal": [
 60    --      { "name": "clk",   "wave": "P......." },
 61    --      { "name": "rst",   "wave": "1.0....." },
 62    --      { "name": "state", "wave": "=.=.....", "data": ["(any)","RED"] },
 63    --      { "name": "red",   "wave": "x.1....." }
 64    --    ]}
 65    p_state_reg : process(clk)
 66    begin
 67        if rising_edge(clk) then
 68            if rst = '1' then
 69                state <= RED;
 70            else
 71                state <= next_state;
 72            end if;
 73        end if;
 74    end process p_state_reg;
 75
 76    -- p_next_state: Combinational next-state logic.
 77    --
 78    -- Evaluates the FSM transition function. Default assignment
 79    -- next_state <= state implements hold when timer_exp is low.
 80    -- All four transitions are guarded solely by timer_exp = 1.
 81    --
 82    -- Transitions:
 83    --   RED       + timer_exp -> RED_AMBER
 84    --   RED_AMBER + timer_exp -> GREEN
 85    --   GREEN     + timer_exp -> AMBER
 86    --   AMBER     + timer_exp -> RED
 87    --
 88    -- .. wavedrom::
 89    --
 90    --    { "signal": [
 91    --      { "name": "clk",       "wave": "P........." },
 92    --      { "name": "timer_exp", "wave": "0.1.1.1.1." },
 93    --      { "name": "state",     "wave": "=.=.=.=.=.",
 94    --        "data": ["RED","RED_AMB","GREEN","AMBER","RED"] },
 95    --      { "name": "red_out",   "wave": "1.1.0.0.1." },
 96    --      { "name": "amber_out", "wave": "0.1.0.1.0." },
 97    --      { "name": "green_out", "wave": "0.0.1.0.0." }
 98    --    ]}
 99    p_next_state : process(state, timer_exp)
100    begin
101        next_state <= state;
102
103        case state is
104            when RED =>
105                if timer_exp = '1' then
106                    next_state <= RED_AMBER;
107                end if;
108
109            when RED_AMBER =>
110                if timer_exp = '1' then
111                    next_state <= GREEN;
112                end if;
113
114            when GREEN =>
115                if timer_exp = '1' then
116                    next_state <= AMBER;
117                end if;
118
119            when AMBER =>
120                if timer_exp = '1' then
121                    next_state <= RED;
122                end if;
123        end case;
124    end process p_next_state;
125
126    -- p_outputs: Combinational Moore output decode.
127    --
128    -- Drives lamp outputs based solely on current state.
129    -- All outputs default to 0 before the case statement to
130    -- prevent latches during synthesis.
131    --
132    -- Output truth table:
133    --   RED       -> red=1 amber=0 green=0
134    --   RED_AMBER -> red=1 amber=1 green=0
135    --   GREEN     -> red=0 amber=0 green=1
136    --   AMBER     -> red=0 amber=1 green=0
137    --
138    -- .. wavedrom::
139    --
140    --    { "signal": [
141    --      { "name": "state",     "wave": "=.=.=.=.",
142    --        "data": ["RED","RED_AMB","GREEN","AMBER"] },
143    --      { "name": "red_out",   "wave": "1.1.0.0." },
144    --      { "name": "amber_out", "wave": "0.1.0.1." },
145    --      { "name": "green_out", "wave": "0.0.1.0." }
146    --    ]}
147    p_outputs : process(state)
148    begin
149        red_out   <= '0';
150        amber_out <= '0';
151        green_out <= '0';
152
153        case state is
154            -- define red
155            when RED       => red_out <= '1';
156            when RED_AMBER => red_out <= '1'; amber_out <= '1';
157            when GREEN     => green_out <= '1';
158            when AMBER     => amber_out <= '1';
159        end case;
160    end process p_outputs;
161
162end architecture rtl;
Previous Next
